title: Blocked Admin Access After ACL Update

ticket.summary: Network admins report they can no longer SSH into core-sw1 (10.10.1.1) from the management subnet (10.10.99.0/24) after a colleague updated an ACL yesterday to 'tighten security'. All other traffic seems fine.

topology.description: mgmt-workstation (10.10.99.50) connects to core-sw1's management VLAN (VLAN 99, IP 10.10.1.1) to reach the rest of the network for SSH administration. An ACL named MGMT_ACL controls which management-subnet hosts may reach core-sw1.

cli_outputs.output:
Standard IP access list MGMT_ACL
    10 permit 10.10.99.100
    20 deny any log

monitoring.notes: SSH attempts from 10.10.99.50 all show as denied in the last 24h; no denies before yesterday's ACL change.

hints: Compare the source IP in the deny log (10.10.99.50) against what the ACL actually permits.
Standard ACLs only match source IP — the colleague likely permitted the wrong single host instead of the whole subnet.
Line 10 permits only 10.10.99.100, not the /24 — everything else in that subnet, including .50, falls through to the explicit deny.

solution.root_cause: The updated ACL only permits a single host (10.10.99.100) instead of the full management subnet (10.10.99.0/24), so all other admin workstations are denied by the implicit/explicit deny.
solution.fix: Replace the host-specific permit line with a subnet-based permit, e.g. 'permit 10.10.99.0 0.0.0.255', so the entire authorized management subnet is allowed.
